steps.title: Divide by package size
steps.content:
Each package has $14$ rolls, and $73$ rolls are needed. Divide to find how many packages would be required before rounding.
$$\frac{73}{14}=5.214\dots$$

steps.title: Round up to packages
steps.content: A fraction of a package cannot be bought, and $5$ packages gives only $70$ rolls. The next whole number of packages gives enough rolls. The answer is 6.
